title: June 29 Board Minutes Approval
deliverable: Resolved edits and approval vote for the June 29 board minutes.
context: Draft board minutes are circulating for edits and comments. They are source evidence now, but they are not final until approved.
nextSteps: Collect comments on the draft board minutes.
Resolve edits and clarify any open points.
Place the approval vote on the next team call.
Notify Andrew and the full board once comments are resolved.
prompt: Create a concise DSG board-minute follow-up brief. Include context that the June 29 minutes are still draft until approved, owner, support, approver, due date, save location, immediate next step, and a short message asking the board to resolve comments before the next team call. Keep it factual, team-safe, and free of private notes.

title: NY Charities Registration and Fee
approves: Andrew for submission/payment; Board for governance issues
due: As soon as portal allows filing
deliverable: Filed NY Charities registration/payment confirmation or clear blocker note saved in Finance.
context: Board authorized the NY Charities registration and $25 fee. Portal was down during the meeting; Andrew may pay personally and handle reimbursement or deposit later.
nextSteps: Retry filing/payment when the NY Charities portal allows.
Save confirmation, receipt, or blocker note immediately.
Notify Clark and the board once filed or if blocked.
Keep the update clear, official, and receipt-first.
prompt: Create a DSG compliance task brief for NY Charities registration and fee. Include the board authorization, portal blocker, owner, support, approval path, due timing, save location, who to notify, and a short status update template. Keep the tone official, clear, and receipt-first.

title: ST-119.2 Filing Authority and Packet
tool: Compliance calendar + board agenda + board minutes + ST-119.2 form
deliverable: Confirmed filing authority and assembled ST-119.2 packet only after approval.
context: Agenda and compliance calendar reference ST-119.2, and the Finance folder now contains the form. Draft minutes clearly record the internal rule but not the filing authorization.
nextSteps: Confirm whether filing authority is already approved.
Do not imply approval until confirmed.
Assemble required attachments only after approval.
Notify the full board when clarification is ready.
prompt: Create a DSG ST-119.2 filing authority brief. Include what is known, what is not approved yet, owner, support, approver, due timing, save location, and a checklist for confirming authority before assembling the filing packet. Keep it factual and do not imply approval until confirmed.

title: July Workshop Registrant Care
tool: Email + Zeffy / registration records + Google Sheet if needed
deliverable: Direct registrant messages offering 1-on-1 session, transfer, or refund, with responses tracked.
saveFinalIn: DSG Share Folder > Programming > Workshop Email Communication
context: Registration and revenue are below break-even. Board direction is direct registrant care: 1-on-1 session, transfer, or refund. No broad public cancellation announcement unless Andrew approves exact wording.
nextSteps: Pull the registrant list and payment records.
Confirm the options available to each registrant.
Draft direct messages from Richard or the appropriate sender.
Route final communication to Monae for review before sending.
Track responses and selected options.
prompt: Create a DSG registrant-care email packet for July workshop registrants. The message should be warm, honest, grounded, direct, and clear. Offer three options: 1-on-1 session, future workshop transfer, or refund. Do not create a broad public cancellation announcement. Return plain text copy, branded HTML, subject line, preview text, review checklist, and response-tracking notes.

title: Compliance Calendar Review and Vote
due: Next team call or earlier e-vote
tool: Google Sheets + team call or e-vote
deliverable: Review packet, comments, and captured vote result for the compliance calendar.
context: Compliance calendar was tabled for virtual/electronic review and vote. It is not adopted yet.
nextSteps: Send the compliance calendar review packet.
Collect board comments.
Set or confirm the vote deadline.
Capture the vote result clearly.
prompt: Create a DSG compliance calendar review and vote packet. Include context that the calendar has not been adopted yet, owner, support, approver, deadline, review instructions, comment capture, and vote language. Keep it procedural, team-safe, and clear.

title: Role Clarity, Decision Trees, and Org Chart
approves: Andrew for operational proposal; Board if governance changes
deliverable: Draft role descriptions, decision trees, and master org chart.
context: Team needs clearer operating roles before heavier tooling or future program expansion. Omari's transition-doc search task was already marked Complete and archived; the draft role/org deliverable remains open.
nextSteps: Draft role descriptions.
Draft decision trees.
Prepare a master org chart.
Send to Andrew when ready for first review.
Escalate to board only if governance changes are involved.
prompt: Create a DSG role clarity work plan. Include role descriptions, decision trees, master org chart, owner, support, approval path, due date, save location, and review sequence. Keep it practical, role-clear, and easy for a non-technical team to follow.

title: July-Cycle Post-Mortem and Marketing Funnel Review
approves: Andrew for meeting plan; Board for budget/strategy commitments
tool: Google Docs or Google Sheets + Buffer/social data
deliverable: Scheduled review meeting, funnel metrics, expert-cost options, and next-cycle decision notes.
context: Before future workshop dates, DSG needs a clean review of what happened and whether outside marketing support is needed.
nextSteps: Schedule the review meeting this week.
Collect registration metrics.
Collect Buffer, social, Manychat, and outreach notes.
Identify expert-cost options if outside support is needed.
Capture decisions and next actions.
prompt: Create a DSG July-cycle post-mortem and marketing funnel review packet. Include agenda, metrics to collect, outreach/social review, audience engagement questions, expert-cost options, owner, support, approval path, save location, and decision log. Internal tone should be direct and factual. External language must follow DSG brand rules.

title: Finance/Admin Cleanup
approves: Clark for finance records; Andrew for admin accounts
tool: Relay + Zeffy + Google Workspace Admin + Goodstack + Zoom + Amazon
deliverable: Resolved or parked status for Relay receipts, Zeffy ACH, ST-119.2 packet materials, Amazon redelivery, and admin platform items.
context: Relay receipts, Zeffy ACH, ST-119.2 packet materials, Amazon redelivery, and admin platform items need confirmation or parking.
nextSteps: Upload or forward Relay receipts.
Reconcile Zeffy ACH.
Verify platform account items.
Confirm Amazon redelivery if needed.
Document blockers without placing credentials in shared docs.
prompt: Create a DSG finance/admin cleanup checklist. Include Relay receipts, Zeffy ACH, ST-119.2 packet materials, Amazon redelivery, platform accounts, owner, support, approval path, due date, save location, and a safe note that credentials should not be saved in shared docs.

title: Funding Readiness
support: Omari / Vaughn if guidance call moves forward
deliverable: Staged IRS letter, budget, program description, metrics, equity narrative, concept note, and CitizensNYC/NYCT fit notes.
context: Funding leads are active but should not displace urgent board/workshop follow-through. CitizensNYC is open through July 27; NYC Future Fund is loan financing; identity and sponsor-benefit language need approval before external use.
nextSteps: Stage IRS letter, budget, and program description.
Stage metrics, equity narrative, and concept note.
Prepare CitizensNYC and NYCT fit notes.
Flag identity or sponsor-benefit language before external use.
Save materials in the fundraising folder.
prompt: Create a DSG funding readiness brief. Include active leads, what not to displace, CitizensNYC deadline, NYC Future Fund loan-financing note, owner, support, board review triggers, save location, and checklist for IRS letter, budget, program description, metrics, equity narrative, concept note, and fit notes.

title: Team Next-Steps Feedback and Blocker Capture
support: Omari / team leads if Andrew delegates reply capture
deliverable: Logged team replies, edits, blockers, assignment questions, and evidence-backed updates to assignments or brief needs.
context: Andrew sent the Team Next Steps email on June 29. The next job is collecting replies, edits, blockers, and assignment questions so the cockpit reflects what the team actually says.
nextSteps: Review team replies to the Team Next Steps email.
Capture blockers, assignment edits, and questions.
Update the cockpit only when new evidence supports the change.
Avoid raw private notes; keep summaries clear, direct, warm, and accountable.
prompt: Create a DSG blocker-capture workflow for team replies to the Team Next Steps email. Include what to log, what not to log, owner/support/approver, due timing, evidence rules, and a short update format for blockers, assignment changes, and decisions needed before the next team call.
